name: Trifle 2
recipeInstructions:
This is a wonderful recipe. It calls for frozen fruit, but you could substitute fresh fruit if available.
Thaw and drain the fruit. Slice the sponge cake into three thin layers. Place one layer in the bottom of a serving bowl. It should be flat-bottomed and about 10 inches across and five inches deep. If using glass, it makes the layers look very pretty. Sprinkle the cake layer with one-quarter cup sherry. Spread about one-third of the raspberries and peach slices on top. Pour one-third of the custard over the fruit.
Repeat the cake, sherry, fruit, and custard layers, saving a dozen or so raspberries for garnish. To make the topping, beat egg whites until stiff and whip cream in a separate bowl. Stir sugar into the cream and gently fold in the beaten egg whites. Mound the cream and egg white mixture over the top of the trifle.
Garnish with raspberries and toasted almonds. Refrigerate until ready to serve.
Custard Sauce: Scald milk in a double boiler. Meanwhile, beat egg yolks and add sugar and salt. Pour a little hot milk into the egg yolk mixture, beating with a fork, and then stir into the rest of the milk. Place over simmering (not boiling) water and cook, stirring until the mixture coats a metal spoon. Stir in vanilla. Cover and refrigerate for one hour or until thickened before adding to the trifle.
